bead.title: test: add executable @covers traceability scanner
bead.description:
Context: TP-001 §3 and §13 require parseable executable citations in the form `@covers US-<n>-AC<m>`. Current executable Rust/UI/SDK tests contain zero canonical `@covers` tags while STPs list many planned citations. Add a scanner so autonomous runs can prove coverage traceability instead of relying on manual review.

In-scope files: a small script or test under the repo's existing tooling, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, and docs only if a command is documented.
Out-of-scope: adding the actual missing citations or new product behavior; those are separate beads.
Implementation hint: scan `crates/`, `ui/`, and `sdk/typescript/` for canonical citations; parse `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/STP-*.md`; fail on malformed tags and optionally report uncited planned AC IDs without requiring all planned IDs to be closed in this bead.
bead.acceptance:
1. A repo command exists that scans executable tests for canonical `@covers US-<n>-AC<m>` citations and exits non-zero on malformed citation syntax.
2. The scanner reports counts by AC ID and by subsystem.
3. CI runs the scanner.
4. `cargo test`, `cd ui && bun run check:story-coverage`, and `cd sdk/typescript && npm test -- --run` still pass.
